_templateName: Handle e-commerce support on Telegram with Gemini and Google Sheets
_templateDescription:
Telegram E-Commerce Customer Support Bot with AI Agent & Google Sheets

An AI-powered Telegram bot that automates customer support for e-commerce stores — handling order tracking, order cancellations, and support ticket creation, all without human intervention.

Built with a Gemini AI Agent + Simple Memory, the bot understands natural language, remembers the conversation context per user, and reads/writes directly to your Google Sheets — no database or backend required.

How It Works

A customer messages the Telegram bot or taps a menu button
The AI Agent (powered by Google Gemini) reads the message and decides what to do
Simple Memory** keeps track of the full conversation per user, so the bot never forgets context mid-flow
The agent calls one of three Google Sheets tools depending on intent:
  Read Orders Sheet — looks up order details by Order ID or email
  Update Order Status — sets an order's status to Cancelled after confirmation
  Create Support Ticket — appends a new ticket row to the support sheet with auto-categorisation
The response is sent back to the user via Telegram

How to Use

Step 1 — Create a Telegram Bot
Open @BotFather on Telegram, run /newbot, and copy your bot token. Add it as a Telegram credential in n8n.

Step 2 — Set Up Google Sheets

Create a Google Spreadsheet with two sheets (tabs):

Sheet 1 — Sheet1 (Orders):
| order_id | customer_name | email | product | Status | date |

Sheet 2 — Sheet1 (Support Tickets — separate spreadsheet):
| ticket_id | name | order_id | query | summary | category | status | created_at | telegram_id |

Step 3 — Add Credentials in n8n
Telegram Bot** — paste your BotFather token
Google Sheets OAuth2** — connect your Google account
Google Gemini (PaLM API)** — add your API key from Google AI Studio

Step 4 — Update Sheet IDs
In the three Google Sheets tool nodes, replace the documentId values with your actual spreadsheet IDs (found in the Google Sheets URL).

Step 5 — Activate & Register Webhook
Activate the workflow in n8n. If running locally, use ngrok to expose your n8n port over HTTPS, then register the webhook:
https://api.telegram.org/bot<YOUR_TOKEN>/setWebhook?url=https://<YOUR_NGROK_URL>/webhook/telegram-ecom-bot

Step 6 — Test
Open your bot in Telegram and send /start. You should see the welcome message with three menu buttons.

Requirements

n8n (self-hosted or cloud)
Telegram Bot (via BotFather — free)
Google account with access to Google Sheets
Google Gemini API key (free tier available at aistudio.google.com)

Customising This Workflow

Change the LLM** — swap Google Gemini for OpenAI GPT-4o or any other n8n-supported model by replacing the Google Gemini Chat Model node
Add more menu options** — extend the /start welcome node with additional inline keyboard buttons and update the system prompt to handle new intents
Add manager notifications** — connect a Telegram or email node after the Create Support Ticket tool to notify your team when a new ticket is raised
Use a database instead of Sheets** — replace the Google Sheets tool nodes with PostgreSQL or MySQL nodes for higher-volume production use
Change cancellation rules** — edit the system prompt to block cancellations for different statuses (e.g. block Ready in addition to Shipped)
Add order lookup by phone number** — extend the orders sheet with a phone column and update the system prompt accordingly

parameters.text: ={{ '👋 Hello *' + $json.firstName + '*! Welcome to our Support Bot.\n\nHow can I help you today?' }}

additionalFields.callback_data: I want to cancel my order
I need to talk to support

options.systemMessage:
You are a friendly e-commerce customer support bot. The customer's name is {{ $('Extract Message').first().json.firstName }} and their Telegram chat ID is {{ $('Extract Message').first().json.chatId }}.

You have 3 tools available:
- **Tool: Read Orders Sheet** — reads ALL order rows. Use to find an order by order_id or email.
- **Tool: Update Order Status** — updates an order's Status column. Use to cancel orders.
- **Tool: Create Support Ticket** — appends a new ticket row to the support sheet.

---

## HOW TO HANDLE EACH INTENT

### "Track my order" / "order status":
1. Ask: "Please provide your Order ID or email address."
2. Call **Tool: Read Orders Sheet** — it returns all rows.
3. Filter rows to find where order_id or email matches what the user gave.
4. Reply with:
```
📦 *Order Details*

🆔 Order ID: [order_id]
👤 Customer: [customer_name]
🛍 Product: [product]
⏳ Status: [Status]
📅 Date: [date]
```
If not found: "❌ No order found. Please check your Order ID or email."

### "Cancel my order":
1. Ask for Order ID or email if not provided.
2. Call **Tool: Read Orders Sheet** to find the order.
3. Check the Status:
   - "Shipped" → "⚠️ Cannot cancel — order already shipped. Please contact support."
   - "Cancelled" → "ℹ️ This order is already cancelled."
   - Any other status → Ask: "⚠️ Are you sure you want to cancel Order [order_id] (Status: [Status])? Reply YES to confirm or NO to keep it."
4. On YES: Call **Tool: Update Order Status** with order_id and Status = "Cancelled". Then reply: "✅ Order [order_id] has been cancelled successfully."
5. On NO: "👍 No problem! Your order has not been cancelled."

### "Talk to support" / "I have an issue":
1. Ask for: Name, and their query/issue. Also ask for Order ID if relevant.
2. Once you have name + query, call **Tool: Create Support Ticket**.
3. After the tool call, YOU MUST reply with this message (replace the values):
   "🎫 *Support Ticket Created!*

✅ Thank you, [name]!

📋 *Your issue:* [one sentence summary]
🏷 *Category:* [Payment/Shipping/Product Issue/Other]

🎟 *Ticket ID:* [the ticket_id you used]

Our team will contact you soon. Type /start to go back to the menu."

---

## CRITICAL RULES
- After calling any tool, ALWAYS send a text reply to the user. Never end without a message.
- Never say "I have submitted" without also telling the user their ticket ID.
- Keep replies concise and friendly.
- Use Markdown formatting (*bold*, _italic_).
- If user greets or types something unclear, list the 3 options they can choose from.

parameters.toolDescription: Reads ALL rows from the orders Google Sheet. Returns all order data including order_id, customer_name, email, product, Status, date. Use this whenever you need to look up an order by order_id or email address.

value.Status: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('Status', 'The new status. Use Cancelled to cancel an order.', 'string') }}

parameters.toolDescription: Updates the Status of an order in the orders sheet. Use this to cancel an order after user confirms. Provide the order_id and set Status to 'Cancelled'.

value.name: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('name', 'Customer full name', 'string') }}
value.category: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('category', 'Issue category: Payment, Shipping, Product Issue, or Other', 'string') }}
value.order_id: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('order_id', 'Related order ID if any, otherwise empty string', 'string') }}
value.ticket_id: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('ticket_id', 'Unique ticket ID in format TKT-XXXX where XXXX is 4 random digits', 'string') }}

parameters.toolDescription: Creates a new support ticket by appending a row to the support sheet. Use this after collecting customer name and query. Generate a unique ticket_id like TKT-XXXX (random 4 digits). Fill summary with a 1-sentence description. Set category to one of: Payment, Shipping, Product Issue, Other. Set status to Pending.

parameters.content:
### ⚙️ Setup Required
1. Add your **Telegram Bot** credential
2. Add **Google Gemini API** credential
3. Add **Google Sheets OAuth2** credential
4. Update Sheet IDs if needed
5. Activate workflow and register webhook

###  🤖 Telegram AI Support Bot

This workflow manages customer interactions via Telegram, using an AI Agent to handle order tracking, cancellations, and support tickets automatically.

###  Step-1: Inbound & Extraction


Receives messages or button clicks and cleans the data (Chat ID, Name, Text) for the rest of the flow.

###  Steo-2: Logic Router

Checks if the user typed **/start**.

- **True:** Shows the main menu buttons.
- **False:** Passes the query to the AI Agent.

###  Step-3: AI Agent & Google Sheets Tools

The **Gemini-powered Agent** uses context and memory to decide which tool to use:
- **Read Orders:** To track status.
- **Update Orders:** To handle cancellations.
- **Create Ticket:** To log issues in the support sheet."

###  Step-4: Response Delivery


Formats the AI output into a user-friendly message and sends it back to Telegram.
